response:
# Verdict: REVISE ## Overall Assessment This accordion component has **critical accessibility design gaps** that pass automated tests but fail real screen reader users. The panel lacks proper region semantics (`role="region"`), missing `aria-labelledby` association to its trigger button, and no heading structure for content organization. These issues create a fragmented experience where screen readers announce each item as generic text blocks rather than logical regions with clear relationships between controls and their panels. ## Pre-commitment Predictions (Before Reading Code) | Prediction | Actual Finding | |------------|-----------------| | Accordion buttons have aria-expanded ✓ | Confirmed present at line 12 | | Panels reference via id/aria-controls ✓ | Correctly implemented at lines 13, 16 | | Missing role="region" on panels ⚠️ | **Confirmed** — critical gap found | | Missing aria-labelledby association ⚠️ | **Confirmed** — major gap found | | No heading structure for items ⚠️ | **Confirmed** — semantic gap | ## Critical Findings (Blocks Access) ### A11y Evidence Finding ```json { "finding_id": "accordion-region-001", "fingerprint": "a8b3c9d2e7f4g5h6i1j0k9l8m7n6o5p4q3r2s1t0u9v8w7x6y5z4" } ``` **CRITICAL: Panel missing role="region"** — See `src/components/Accordion.tsx:17-20` where the panel is a plain `
` without semantic region designation. Per **WAI-ARIA APG Disclosure pattern**, content panels should have either `role="region"` with appropriate landmark semantics OR be wrapped in native heading elements (`

`, etc.). Screen readers announce this as generic text blocks, not logical expandable regions. **User Group Impacted:** Screen reader users (NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver) - **WCAG Citation:** 1.3.1 Info and Relationships; WAI-ARIA APG Disclosure pattern - **Expected Behavior:** Panel should be announced as "region" or have heading semantics with clear relationship to trigger button via `aria-labelledby` - **Actual Behavior:** Screen reader announces content without indicating it's part of an accordion panel structure **Fix:** Add `
` OR wrap in appropriate heading element. The `aria-labelledby` should reference the button that controls this region (via its ID or accessible name). --- ### A11y Evidence Finding ```json { "finding_id": "accordion-label-002", "fingerprint": "b9c4d8e3f6g7h5i2j1k0l9m8n7o6p5q4r3s2t1u0v9w8x7y6z5a4" } ``` **CRITICAL: Missing aria-labelledby association between button and panel** — See `src/components/Accordion.tsx:12-16`. The trigger button has text content but the panel doesn't reference it via `aria-labelledby`, nor does the button have an ID for reverse referencing. Per **WAI-ARIA APG Disclosure pattern**, each panel must be programmatically associated with its controlling element so screen readers announce "panel, region" when expanded and know which control manages it. **User Group Impacted:** Screen reader users - **WCAG Citation:** 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value; WAI-ARIA APG Disclosure pattern - **Expected Behavior:** Panel should have `aria-labelledby={`accordion-trigger-${index}`} pointing to button ID` so screen readers announce the region with its controlling label when expanded **Fix:** Add unique IDs to buttons (e.g., `id={`accordion-trigger-${index}`}`) and add `aria-labelledby={buttonId} id={`panel-${index}`}>` on panels. --- ## Major Findings (Significantly Degrades Experience) ### A11y Evidence Finding ```json { "finding_id": "accordion-heading-003", "fingerprint": "c7d6e5f4g8h9i2j3k4l5m6n7o8p9q0r1s2t3u4v5w6x7y8z9a0b" } ``` **MAJOR: Missing heading structure for accordion items** — See `src/components/Accordion.tsx:1-2`. Accordion content should use appropriate heading levels (`

`, `

` depending on page hierarchy) rather than plain text inside buttons. Per **WCAG 1.3.1**, headings establish document outline and navigation landmarks for screen reader users who rely on heading jumps to navigate sections. **User Group Impacted:** Screen reader, keyboard-only users - **WCAG Citation:** 2.4.6 Headings and Labels; WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships - **Expected Behavior:** Use `

` or appropriate level for accordion item titles inside button with `aria-labelledby` pointing to heading element **Fix:** Restructure as: ```jsx
{expanded === index && ( <>

{item.title}

{/* Or keep title in button if heading is separate */} {item.content} )}
``` --- ### A11y Evidence Finding ```json { "finding_id": "accordion-focus-004", "fingerprint": "d8e9f7g6h5i4j3k2l1m0n9o8p7q6r5s4t3u2v1w0x9y8z7a6b" } ``` **MAJOR: Focus management not documented or implemented** — See `src/components/Accordion.tsx`. When a user tabs through accordion items, focus should move to the next button. However, there's no evidence of: (1) roving tabindex implementation for arrow-key navigation within an accordion group, OR (2) proper handling when multiple accordions exist on same page. Per **WAI-ARIA APG Disclosure pattern**, if keyboard users can navigate between items with arrows, focus should move to the next item in sequence and announce state changes appropriately. **User Group Impacted:** Keyboard-only users - **WCAG Citation:** 2.1.1 Keyboard; WAI-ARIA APG Tabs/Disclosure patterns - **Expected Behavior:** Arrow keys navigate between accordion items (if multiple per page), Enter/Space toggles expansion, Escape closes expanded panel **Fix:** Implement arrow key handlers for multi-item accordions: ```jsx const handleArrowDown = ()
